summary: cookie-parser-js fetches and eval's remote JavaScript from a base64-hidden endpoint
details: Package impersonates the legitimate `cookie-parser` (reusing its author metadata, repository field `expressjs/cookie-parser`, README, and API) and adds `axios` as a dependency to carry a payload fetch. Inside the exported middleware factory, the code decodes a base64 literal via `atob` to `https://cookie-api-two.vercel.app/`, GETs that URL through axios, and passes the response `content` field to `eval`. Any Express application that requires this package and installs the middleware executes attacker-controlled JavaScript inside the Node server process, giving the operator of that endpoint arbitrary remote code execution in the request-handling process. The base64 encoding of the destination URL hides the C2 from casual inspection.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
